proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher feature_015 is associated with longer pfs_months across the cohort.
feature_001 ordinal level (0/1/2) is negatively associated with pfs_months: higher levels have lower mean pfs_months.
feature_014 = 1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than feature_014 = 0.

analyses.code:
from scipy import stats
stats.pearsonr(df['feature_015'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Strong positive Pearson correlation r=0.766 between feature_015 and pfs_months (p<1e-300). Mean pfs_months by feature_015 quintile rises monotonically from 1.86 (lowest) to 6.83 (highest).
Mean pfs_months: 5.27 (f001=0), 4.05 (f001=1), 2.91 (f001=2). Dummy regression: feature_001=1 coef=-1.22 (p<1e-300), feature_001=2 coef=-2.36 (p<1e-300). Strong monotone negative gradient.
Mean pfs_months 3.70 (f014=1) vs 5.05 (f014=0); Welch t-test p<1e-300. Difference = -1.35 months.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_027 = 1 is associated with longer pfs_months than feature_027 = 0; given its 20% prevalence and positive effect, feature_027 is a candidate treatment indicator.
feature_023 = 1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than feature_023 = 0.
feature_006 = 1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than feature_006 = 0.

analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months 5.09 (f027=1) vs 4.12 (f027=0); difference +0.97 months, Welch t p~1.7e-216. 20% of patients have feature_027=1.
Mean pfs_months 4.18 (f023=1) vs 4.50 (f023=0); difference -0.33, p~1.9e-58.
Mean pfs_months 4.20 (f006=1) vs 4.50 (f006=0); difference -0.31, p~1.0e-49.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_005 (right-skewed lab; mean ~9, max 777) is negatively associated with pfs_months.
feature_019 (continuous, mean ~3.8) is positively associated with pfs_months.
feature_025 is negatively associated with pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Pearson r = -0.038, p~3.1e-17. Negative association though magnitude small on linear scale (highly skewed distribution).
Pearson r = +0.102, p~3.2e-115. Strong positive prognostic signal.
Pearson r = -0.123, p~2.2e-167. Strong negative prognostic signal.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable OLS adjusting for all 33 features, feature_027 retains a positive coefficient on pfs_months (treatment-like signal independent of confounders).
feature_031 (binary, 45% prevalence) has no independent association with pfs_months after adjustment.
feature_011, feature_002, and feature_028 (rare binaries with prevalence 3%, 0.5%, 3%) have no significant adjusted association with pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: feature_027 adjusted coef +0.943 (p<1e-300). feature_031 adjusted coef +0.010 (p=0.19, ns). feature_011 +0.020 (p=0.37 ns), feature_002 +0.039 (p=0.47 ns), feature_028 -0.016 (p=0.48 ns). Strongest adjusted effects: feature_015 +0.176 per unit, feature_001 -1.171, feature_014 -1.362, feature_027 +0.943, feature_019 +0.469, feature_025 -0.076, feature_023 -0.354, feature_022 -0.356, feature_006 -0.287.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_027 is the principal candidate treatment in this cohort (binary, ~20% prevalence, positive adjusted effect on pfs_months).
The marginal +0.94 month treatment effect of feature_027 hides substantial heterogeneity: at least one binary modifier exists where feature_027 effect drops to ~0.

analyses.result_summary: 10,022 of 50,000 patients (20.0%) have feature_027=1. Marginal effect on pfs_months = +0.97 months (p~1.7e-216). Adjusted for all other features = +0.94 months (p<1e-300). Consistent with a binary treatment indicator.

analyses.code: Stratified ttest_ind for feature_027 within strata of every other binary feature
analyses.result_summary: Stratification reveals: feature_006=0 stratum d=+1.48 (p~3.7e-274) vs feature_006=1 stratum d=+0.03 (p=0.52). feature_023=0 stratum d=+1.66 (p~6.1e-293) vs feature_023=1 stratum d=+0.03 (p=0.46). Strong heterogeneity confirmed.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Multiplicative interaction feature_027 x feature_006 is negative: feature_006=1 abolishes the feature_027 PFS benefit.
Multiplicative interaction feature_027 x feature_023 is negative: feature_023=1 abolishes the feature_027 PFS benefit.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef = -1.454 (p~4.1e-168). In model with f027, f023 and both interactions: f027 coef +2.17, f027xf006 coef -1.47 (p~1e-300), f027xf023 coef -1.64 (p~1e-300).
Interaction coef = -1.629 (p~7.7e-226). Independent of feature_006, this term is similarly large and negative.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Systematic interaction screen feature_027 x every other feature will identify additional modifiers beyond feature_006 and feature_023.

analyses.code: For each feature f: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_027 + f + feature_027:f, record interaction p-value
analyses.result_summary: Top interactions: feature_023 (b=-1.63, p~7.7e-226), feature_006 (b=-1.45, p~4.1e-168), feature_005 (b=-0.025/unit, p~8.7e-58), feature_022 (b=-1.18, p~6.1e-23), feature_011 (b=+0.99, p~1.2e-11). All other features: p>0.10. Modifiers identified: feature_006, feature_023, feature_022 (binary, suppress effect when =1), feature_005 (continuous, suppresses effect at high values), and feature_011 (binary, possibly enhances effect when =1).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_027 x feature_022 interaction is negative: feature_022=1 abolishes the feature_027 PFS benefit.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_027 + feature_022 + feature_027:feature_022; stratified ttest
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef = -1.18 (p~6.1e-23). Stratified: feature_022=0 stratum n=47728, f027 effect d=+1.06 (p<1e-300); feature_022=1 stratum n=2272, f027 effect d=-0.20 (small/null).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_027 x feature_005 interaction is negative: as feature_005 increases, the PFS benefit of feature_027 declines toward zero.
A natural threshold near feature_005=5 (likely a clinical upper-limit cutoff) cleanly separates feature_027 responders (f005<=5: large benefit) from non-responders (f005>5: ~no benefit).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef = -0.0253 per unit feature_005 (p~8.7e-58). Continuous attenuation of feature_027 effect by feature_005.

f005>5 dichotomy with interaction: f027 main effect +1.79 (p<1e-300); f027 x I(f005>5) interaction -1.77 (p~1e-275). Stratified: f005<=5 (n=26997) f027 effect d=+1.79; f005>5 (n=23003) f027 effect d=+0.014 (ns).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Joint subgroup feature_006=0 AND feature_023=0 has a far larger feature_027 effect on pfs_months than the rest of the cohort.

analyses.code: Stratified ttest in (f006=0 & f023=0) vs complement
analyses.result_summary: (f006=0 & f023=0) subgroup (n=18853, 38% of cohort): f027 effect d=+2.520 (p<1e-300; mean 6.64 vs 4.12). Complement (f006=1 OR f023=1, n=31147): f027 effect d=+0.04 (p=0.16, ns). Heterogeneity is strong and orthogonal between the two modifiers.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Adding feature_022=0 to the (f006=0 & f023=0) subgroup further increases the f027 effect estimate.

analyses.result_summary: (f006=0 & f023=0 & f022=0) subgroup (n=17372, 35% of cohort): f027 effect d=+2.758 (p<1e-300). Adding f022=0 increases the effect from +2.52 to +2.76 months.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Adding feature_005<=5 to the (f006=0 & f023=0 & f022=0) subgroup yields the maximum f027 PFS benefit (~5 months) and constitutes the complete responder phenotype.

analyses.code: Stratified ttest in (f006=0 & f023=0 & f022=0 & f005<=5)
analyses.result_summary: Responder subgroup (n=9338, 18.7% of cohort): mean pfs_months 4.09 (f027=0; n=7433) vs 9.10 (f027=1; n=1905). f027 effect d=+5.014 months (Welch t p<1e-300). This is by far the largest treatment effect found in any subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Outside the responder subgroup (f006=1 OR f023=1 OR f022=1 OR f005>5), feature_027 has no detectable PFS benefit.

analyses.code: Stratified ttest in non-responder mask (f006==1 | f023==1 | f022==1 | f005>5)
analyses.result_summary: Non-responder subgroup (n=40662, 81.3% of cohort): f027 effect d=+0.024 months (p=0.36, ns). Confirms heterogeneity is binary in nature: virtually all of the marginal +0.97-month effect of f027 is attributable to the responder subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A jointly fit OLS interaction model (f027 x f006, f027 x f023, f027 x f022, f027 x f005 all in one model) confirms each modifier carries an independent suppressive contribution to the f027 effect.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_027 + f006 + f023 + f022 + f005 + interactions
analyses.result_summary: Joint interaction model coefficients: feature_027 main +2.547 (p<1e-300); f027xf006 -1.472 (p~1e-300); f027xf023 -1.781 (p~1e-300); f027xf022 -1.963 (p~1e-300); f027xf005 -0.024/unit (p~3e-55). Each suppressor effect remains highly significant adjusting for the others; their effects are largely additive.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_011=1 enhances the feature_027 effect (sign of f027 x f011 interaction is positive).

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ f027 + f011 + f027:f011; stratified ttest
analyses.result_summary: Univariate-only interaction: f027 x f011 coef = +0.99 (p~1.2e-11). However, in the full joint model with f006, f023, f022, f005 modifiers added, f027 x f011 coef = +0.16 (p=0.27, ns). Within the responder subgroup (f6=0,f23=0,f22=0,f005<=5), f027 effect is +5.00 in f011=0 (n=8843) and +5.24 in f011=1 (n=495); difference is small. Conclusion: f011 enhancement is not robust after accounting for the major modifiers; do NOT include in final subgroup definition.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A T-learner gradient-boosted CATE estimator coupled with a shallow regression tree on the estimated CATE will recover the (f005<=4.95 & f023=0 & f006=0 & f022=0) responder subgroup as the highest-CATE leaf.

analyses.code: T-learner: GradientBoostingRegressor on f027=1 and f027=0 separately; CATE = mu1(x) - mu0(x); fit DecisionTreeRegressor(max_depth=4, min_samples_leaf=500) on CATE
analyses.result_summary: Tree feature importances for CATE: feature_023 (0.36), feature_006 (0.32), feature_005 (0.26), feature_022 (0.07); all others <0.001. Top leaf: feature_005<=4.95 & feature_023<=0.5 & feature_006<=0.5 & feature_022<=0.5 -> CATE ~ +4.24 months. Every other leaf has CATE between -0.44 and +0.73. Tree-based subgroup discovery is fully consistent with the manually constructed responder phenotype.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_011 is not itself a treatment: in marginal and stratified analyses it has no main effect on pfs_months and no consistent interaction structure.
feature_002 (rare binary, 0.5%) is not a treatment.

analyses.result_summary: Marginal: f011=1 vs 0 d=+0.22 (p~7.3e-04, weakly significant due to confounding with f027). In adjusted OLS f011 coef = +0.020 (p=0.37, ns). Interaction screen for f011 x other features: top interaction with feature_021 (p=0.05); none survive multiple-testing thresholds. Conclusion: prognostic / nuisance variable, not a treatment.
f002=1 vs 0 d=+0.040 months (p=0.79, ns; n1=251). Adjusted coef +0.039 (p=0.47, ns). No treatment signal.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_014 is a strong prognostic variable but does NOT modify the feature_027 treatment effect.
feature_001 (3-level) is a strong prognostic variable but does NOT modify the feature_027 treatment effect across its three levels.

analyses.code: Stratified ttest by feature_014; OLS interaction term
analyses.result_summary: f027 effect: f014=0 d=+0.94 (n=22519); f014=1 d=+1.01 (n=27481). Interaction coef +0.07 (p=0.15, ns). feature_014 is purely prognostic.

analyses.code: Stratified ttest by feature_001; OLS interaction terms (f001==1, f001==2)

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_019 is a strong positive prognostic variable but does NOT modify the feature_027 effect.
feature_025 is a strong negative prognostic variable but does NOT modify the feature_027 effect.

analyses.result_summary: feature_019 main coef +0.469 (p<1e-300; positive prognostic). f027 x f019 interaction p=0.12, ns. Feature is prognostic, not predictive of f027 benefit.
feature_025 main coef -0.076 per unit (p<1e-300; negative prognostic). f027 x f025 interaction p=0.36, ns. Feature is prognostic, not predictive of f027 benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_015 is a strong positive prognostic variable on PFS but is NOT a modifier of the feature_027 treatment effect.
feature_031 has neither prognostic nor predictive value (no main effect, no interaction).

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_027 + feature_015 + feature_027:feature_015; deciles of f015
analyses.result_summary: feature_015 main coef +0.176 per unit (p<1e-300; remarkably linear: deciles span pfs 1.33 to 7.45). f027 x f015 interaction coef -0.0017/unit (p=0.28, ns). Feature_015 is prognostic only.

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind by feature_031; OLS f031 main and interaction
analyses.result_summary: Marginal d=-0.006 (p=0.77, ns). Adjusted coef +0.010 (p=0.19, ns). No interaction with f027 (p=0.55). Feature_031 has no detectable signal.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treating feature_005 continuously rather than dichotomized at 5 yields a smooth dose-response of f027 effect attenuation.

analyses.code: Stratified ttest of f027 across multiple thresholds of feature_005
analyses.result_summary: f027 effect by feature_005 threshold: <=1 d=+1.67 (n=5321), <=3 d=+1.80 (n=18644), <=5 d=+1.79 (n=26997), <=10 d=+1.29 (n=37418), <=20 d=+1.09 (n=44631). >5 d=+0.014 (n=23003). The threshold of ~5 cleanly separates responders from non-responders; below 5 the effect is large and stable, above 5 the effect is essentially absent. The continuous interaction (-0.024/unit, p<1e-50) confirms a graded loss of benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The complete responder subgroup definition for feature_027's PFS benefit is: feature_006=0 AND feature_023=0 AND feature_022=0 AND feature_005<=5; outside this subgroup the treatment effect is null.

analyses.code: Final stratified comparison: responder mask vs non-responder mask
analyses.result_summary: Responder subgroup (n=9338, 18.7% of cohort): f027 effect d=+5.014 months (p<1e-300; mean PFS 4.09 -> 9.10). Non-responder subgroup (n=40662, 81.3%): f027 effect d=+0.024 months (p=0.36, ns). The responder phenotype is well separated. Each of feature_006=1, feature_023=1, feature_022=1, and feature_005>5 independently and approximately additively eliminates the benefit (joint OLS interaction coefficients all p<1e-22).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among 'unfavorable-for-treatment' modifiers, feature_022=1 is the rarest (4.5%) and most aggressive single suppressor when isolated.

analyses.code: Single-modifier stratification across f006, f023, f022 with f005<=5 baseline
analyses.result_summary: Within f005<=5, f027 effect: when f006=0 & f023=0 & f022=0 d=+5.01 (n=9338); when ONLY f022=1 (others 0): n smaller, f027 effect ~0; when ONLY f006=1 (others 0) f027 effect ~0; when ONLY f023=1 (others 0) f027 effect ~0. Each suppressor is sufficient to remove the benefit. f022 is the rarest (4.5%), f006 mid-prevalence (35%), f023 highest (42%). Joint interaction magnitudes (per-unit shift in f027 effect): f022 -1.96, f023 -1.78, f006 -1.47.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After the interaction screen, no remaining feature shows a significant interaction with feature_027 beyond the four already identified (f006, f023, f022, f005); thus the responder definition is complete.

analyses.code: Re-run full f027 x feature interaction screen excluding identified modifiers
analyses.result_summary: Beyond f006, f023, f005, f022, f011, no feature has interaction p<0.10 with f027. Next-strongest p-values are feature_007 (p=0.11), feature_019 (p=0.12), feature_013 (p=0.13), feature_018 (p=0.13). After Bonferroni correction over ~33 features, none of these reach significance. The four identified modifiers fully account for treatment-effect heterogeneity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL HYPOTHESIS: feature_027 is a binary treatment whose benefit on pfs_months is concentrated entirely in patients with feature_006=0 AND feature_023=0 AND feature_022=0 AND feature_005<=5 (the responder phenotype, ~18.7% of patients), with mean pfs_months increasing by approximately +5.0 months in this subgroup; outside the subgroup the treatment effect is zero.

FINAL PROGNOSTIC SYNTHESIS: independent of any treatment effect, longer pfs_months is associated with higher feature_015 (linear), lower feature_001 level, feature_014=0, higher feature_019, lower feature_025, feature_006=0, feature_023=0, feature_022=0, lower feature_005, and lower feature_021. feature_031, feature_010, feature_012, feature_018, feature_020, feature_024, feature_028, feature_002, feature_011, feature_016, feature_007, feature_009, feature_033, feature_026, feature_029, feature_003, feature_030, feature_008, feature_004, feature_013, feature_017, feature_032 show no robust association.

analyses.code: Final confirmation: stratified ttest in responder vs non-responder subgroups
analyses.result_summary: Responder subgroup (f006=0 & f023=0 & f022=0 & f005<=5; n=9338): mean pfs_months 4.09 (n0=7433) vs 9.10 (n1=1905); d=+5.014 months, Welch p<1e-300. Non-responder subgroup (n=40662): d=+0.024, p=0.36 (ns). Hypothesis fully supported.

Adjusted coefficients on pfs_months (months per unit): feature_015 +0.176 (p<1e-300), feature_001 -1.171 (p<1e-300), feature_014 -1.362 (p<1e-300), feature_027 +0.943 (p<1e-300; treatment), feature_019 +0.469 (p<1e-300), feature_025 -0.076 (p<1e-300), feature_023 -0.354 (p<1e-300), feature_022 -0.356 (p~4e-79), feature_006 -0.287 (p~1e-272), feature_005 -0.005 (p~2e-97), feature_021 -0.0004 (p~7e-19). Non-significant (p>=0.05): feature_031, feature_011, feature_002, feature_028, feature_016, feature_017, feature_032, feature_024, feature_010, feature_012, feature_018, feature_020, feature_007, feature_003, feature_030, feature_008, feature_004, feature_013, feature_026, feature_029, feature_033, feature_009.
